scenario: A team built a custom Connector Template for "Slack POST." They uploaded it to their project. They wonder how to publish it to organisation scope so all projects in the org can use it.
question: What's the workflow for publishing a Connector Template to organisation scope?

options.text: All Connector Templates are automatically visible to the entire organisation by default -- this misrepresents the default visibility scope. Templates created in a Web Modeler project are project-scoped by default, accessible only to members of that project. Making a template visible to all organisational projects requires an explicit admin-level publishing action to promote the template from project scope to organisation scope.

Distributing copies to each project creates maintenance burden: every update must be applied to each copy separately, increasing the risk of version drift across teams. The org-level publishing feature exists specifically to avoid this copy-and-maintain problem by providing a single canonical version accessible to all projects.

Verify per Web Modeler version -- modern versions typically expose a "Publish to organisation" action (or similar) on the template, requiring an admin role. Once published, the template appears in all projects' template picker. The unpublish / depublish action reverses this. An alternative is to deploy the template programmatically via the Web Modeler API at org scope. Workflow: develop + test the template in a project; verify with a live process; as admin, publish to org; all projects can then use it; maintain centrally as updates propagate.

Connector Templates have no mechanism for organisation-wide sharing and remain confined to individual projects -- this understates the template management capabilities in modern Web Modeler versions. Org-level template publishing allows admins to promote templates from project scope to organisation scope, making them available in the template picker for all organisation members across all projects. Updates to org-scoped templates can be managed centrally and versioned for controlled rollout.

a.text: Incorrect. Modern Web Modeler tiers / versions support template visibility scoping at organisation level. Workflow: Develop the template in a project (test bed where you iterate without affecting other teams). Verify: model a Service Task using the template; deploy; test the Connector works. Publish to organisation: admin user with the right permission promotes the template from project to org scope. The template now appears in all projects' template picker. Maintain: updates to the org-scoped template apply across the org. Versioning supports controlled rollout (deprecate old versions, publish new). Unpublish: admin can demote a template from org scope back to project (or delete). Use case: a centrally-curated library of Connector Templates (Slack, Microsoft Teams, internal-API templates, etc.) that all teams use consistently. Permission model: typically only org admins can publish / unpublish; project users can use published templates. Verify per Web Modeler version + tier -- features evolve.
